package handler

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "errors"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Positive scenario for gin_handler_mixes_html_json_and_file_responses: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: Handlers that serve fundamentally different transport modes from one function.
type CaseGinHandlerMixesHtmlJsonAndFileResponsesDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseGinHandlerMixesHtmlJsonAndFileResponsesModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseGinHandlerMixesHtmlJsonAndFileResponsesRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseGinHandlerMixesHtmlJsonAndFileResponsesModel
}

type CaseGinHandlerMixesHtmlJsonAndFileResponsesService struct {
    repo *CaseGinHandlerMixesHtmlJsonAndFileResponsesRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseGinHandlerMixesHtmlJsonAndFileResponsesAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveGinHandlerMixesHtmlJsonAndFileResponses(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseGinHandlerMixesHtmlJsonAndFileResponsesDTO) error {
    format := c.Query("format")
    switch format {
    case "json":
        c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"data": input})
    case "html":
        c.HTML(http.StatusOK, "template.html", gin.H{"data": input})
    case "file":
        c.File("/tmp/report.csv")
    }
    return nil
}
